label: Customer Cloud Control Plane
current_form: BYOC deployment stack for one security vendor
hidden_wedge: tenant onboarding plus federated observability
end_state_company: deployment operating system for secure SaaS in customer clouds
desperate_user: platform engineering leader at a security vendor
job: launch and support customer-owned deployments without custom DevOps per tenant
workaround: bespoke Terraform, manual cross-account setup, fragile support access
reframing_direction: custom AWS project to reusable BYOC control plane
crazy_upside: default substrate for software that must run inside customer cloud boundaries
reason_alive: Real operational pain, good outcomes, and real leverage if generalized across vendors.

label: Vertica Control Plane
current_form: managed Vertica experience inside customer AWS accounts
hidden_wedge: BYOC warehouse control plane for regulated analytics workloads
end_state_company: customer-cloud operating layer for heavyweight data systems
desperate_user: data platform lead trying to offer managed Vertica without ops drag
buyer: data platform GM or product infrastructure leader
job: make Vertica feel managed without surrendering cloud control
workaround: manual provisioning, hand-built monitoring, slow scaling workflows
reframing_direction: vendor-specific managed experience to broader customer-cloud data control plane
crazy_upside: shared control layer for regulated databases that cannot live in pure SaaS
reason_alive: The wedge is narrow but crisp, and the BYOC pattern is durable.

label: Security Data Labeling
current_form: expert security annotation service
hidden_wedge: security training-data substrate and labeling workflow
end_state_company: canonical data layer for security model development
desperate_user: ML lead at a security vendor starved for trustworthy labeled data
buyer: VP ML or head of threat research
job: turn noisy raw logs into model-ready supervision without burning analyst time
workaround: generic label shops or expensive internal analysts doing annotation
reframing_direction: service labeling to reusable security data asset plus tooling
crazy_upside: ImageNet-like ground-truth corpus for modern threat detection
reason_alive: High-upside data moat if the work becomes a recurring data asset instead of a project.

label: SOC Agent Swarm
current_form: multi-agent SOC investigation stack
hidden_wedge: cross-tool investigation brief generator
end_state_company: vendor-neutral operating layer for security investigations
desperate_user: SOC manager with too many alerts and too few analysts
job: compress investigation time across fragmented consoles and workflows
workaround: manual cross-tool correlation and brittle SOAR playbooks
reframing_direction: agents demo to cross-platform workflow layer
crazy_upside: neutral orchestration brain for multi-vendor SOC environments
reason_alive: The integrations, not the agents, could become the defensible company.

label: Autonomous Driving R&D Platform
current_form: AV research platform bundling MLOps and annotation workflows
hidden_wedge: specialized sensor-fusion annotation pipeline
end_state_company: core tooling layer for vertical autonomy research teams
desperate_user: AV ML lead bottlenecked by multi-sensor labeling and slow iteration
job: turn raw LiDAR, camera, and radar data into usable training loops quickly
workaround: duct-taped MLOps stacks and generic labeling vendors
reframing_direction: broad R&D platform to focused annotation and data workflow wedge
crazy_upside: default data engine for autonomy teams beyond just automotive
reason_alive: The annotation and data-pipeline wedge is sharp enough to matter.

label: AI Vulnerability Remediator
current_form: full vulnerability management platform with AI code fixes
hidden_wedge: scanner-agnostic remediation PR generator
end_state_company: fix layer between security findings and merged code changes
desperate_user: AppSec engineer who cannot get developers to remediate findings fast enough
job: turn findings into safe, reviewable fixes before release cycles stall
workaround: manual triage, ticket spam, and developers rewriting fixes by hand
reframing_direction: big platform to narrow remediation action layer
crazy_upside: default execution engine for vulnerability remediation across the SDLC
reason_alive: The remediation step is painful, budgeted, and still under-automated.

label: SaaS Telemetry Connectors
current_form: connector catalog for SaaS security data ingestion
hidden_wedge: cloud-native telemetry ingestion fabric for detection teams
end_state_company: security data acquisition layer for SaaS-first environments
desperate_user: security platform owner paged when critical SaaS logs stop flowing
job: get identity, email, and endpoint SaaS telemetry into analysis tools reliably
workaround: agents, cron polling, token babysitting, and manual normalization
reframing_direction: connector catalog to reliability layer for telemetry onboarding
crazy_upside: the ingestion backbone every modern SOC depends on before analytics begin
reason_alive: Connector work is commoditized in theory but still operationally ugly in practice.

label: Security Log Normalization Layer
current_form: parser and field-mapping work for security telemetry
hidden_wedge: schema-trust layer that keeps detections from breaking
end_state_company: normalization backbone for security analytics and detection engineering
desperate_user: detection engineering lead whose rules break when new sources arrive
buyer: VP Detection Engineering or XDR platform lead
job: make new telemetry usable quickly without degrading detection quality
workaround: hand-built parsers, stale mappings, and constant false-positive cleanup
reframing_direction: parser services to trusted normalization layer
crazy_upside: security equivalent of Stripe for clean, portable telemetry schemas
reason_alive: The parser itself is thin, but the trusted normalization layer could compound.

label: CMDB Intelligence Layer
current_form: AI enhancement layer for stale enterprise CMDBs
hidden_wedge: dependency mapping and drift detection for one existing CMDB
end_state_company: truth-maintenance layer for enterprise infrastructure records
desperate_user: CMDB owner who knows the system of record is not trustworthy
buyer: IT operations or service management leadership
job: keep the CMDB accurate enough for incidents, audits, and change control
workaround: manual updates, partial discovery tools, and broken dependency maps
reframing_direction: broad AI CMDB story to focused trust-repair workflow
crazy_upside: control point for operational truth across the modern enterprise
reason_alive: Incumbent risk is real, but the trust problem is persistent and valuable.

label: Threat Intel Integration Factory
current_form: framework for rapidly building threat-feed integrations
hidden_wedge: source-onboarding machinery for threat intelligence platforms
end_state_company: operating layer for ingesting and normalizing fragmented threat feeds
desperate_user: platform lead at a threat-intel company adding sources under constant pressure
buyer: head of platform engineering or intel operations
job: turn new feed sources into usable normalized intel without custom engineering every time
workaround: one-off scrapers, brittle transforms, and manual submission pipelines
reframing_direction: integration throughput service to standardized source-factory product
crazy_upside: neutral feed-ingestion layer behind multiple intelligence products
reason_alive: The pain repeats every time a new feed matters, which is exactly what you want.

label: Security Data Lake
current_form: Snowflake-based security lake modernization project
hidden_wedge: financial-services compliance and analytics layer on top of security data plumbing
end_state_company: compliance-native security analytics platform for regulated firms
desperate_user: security or compliance director trying to answer audits across fragmented systems
buyer: CISO or head of compliance technology
job: unify security evidence fast enough for threat response and regulatory reporting
workaround: legacy SIEM fragments, custom ETL, and manual reporting across siloed stores
reframing_direction: generic data lake story to vertical compliance analytics wedge
crazy_upside: system of record for security evidence in regulated industries
reason_alive: The plumbing is absorbable, but the regulated analytics layer still has room.

label: MSSP Control Plane
current_form: multi-tenant AWS platform for one MSSP
hidden_wedge: self-serve partner provisioning layer
end_state_company: control plane for security service providers managing many isolated tenants
desperate_user: head of cloud operations at an MSSP
job: spin up isolated partner and end-customer environments without manual toil
workaround: Terraform heroics, repeated VPC setup, and human bottlenecks in onboarding
reframing_direction: full platform build to narrow partner provisioning product
crazy_upside: Shopify-like operating substrate for managed security providers
reason_alive: Real toil exists, but platform absorption risk is substantial unless the wedge stays narrow.

label: Secure AWS Landing Zone
current_form: repeatable secure AWS environment provisioning project
hidden_wedge: opinionated landing zone for security-sensitive SaaS teams
end_state_company: compliance-first environment factory for regulated startups
desperate_user: platform leader at a security SaaS company facing enterprise onboarding pressure
job: get compliant AWS environments live fast without hand-built infra each time
workaround: manual cloud assembly and consultant-led hardening
reframing_direction: Terraform implementation to compliance opinionated environment product
crazy_upside: standardized launch path for every security-sensitive SaaS vendor
reason_alive: There is some wedge in compliance packaging, but generic landing-zone gravity is heavy.

label: Tenant Ops Control Plane
current_form: internal fleet-management layer for a SaaS ops team
hidden_wedge: Terraform orchestration service for tenant fleets
end_state_company: control plane for operating large fleets of near-identical SaaS environments
desperate_user: cloud operations engineer managing drift across thousands of tenants
job: provision and change customer environments consistently at fleet scale
workaround: semi-automated scripts, manual rollouts, and hidden drift
reframing_direction: internal ops stack to reusable fleet-orchestration product
crazy_upside: base layer for customer-environment fleet management across SaaS
reason_alive: The fleet problem is real, but existing infra-control vendors sit close to it.

label: IT Helpdesk Agent
current_form: enterprise helpdesk chatbot using RAG over internal docs
hidden_wedge: action-taking agent for identity and provisioning tasks
end_state_company: autonomous execution layer for repetitive internal IT operations
desperate_user: IT operations manager buried in repetitive L1 tickets
job: resolve repetitive support requests without adding headcount
workaround: human ticket queues and generic ITSM bots that mostly deflect questions
reframing_direction: FAQ bot to action-taking internal ops agent
crazy_upside: hands-free operating layer for common employee IT workflows
reason_alive: There is a usable wedge only if it executes actions, not if it stays a chatbot.

label: Dynatrace Migration Accelerator
current_form: SPL-to-DQL translation tool embedded in a migration project
hidden_wedge: self-serve query translator for observability migrations
end_state_company: migration utility layer for switching monitoring stacks
desperate_user: observability architect tasked with rewriting hundreds of dashboards fast
job: move alert and dashboard logic without months of manual translation
workaround: consultants or engineers hand-converting queries one by one
reframing_direction: full migration service to narrow translation engine
crazy_upside: Rosetta layer for migration between multiple observability DSLs
reason_alive: The translator is real, but the need is episodic and vendor pull is shaky.

label: Splunk Cloud Replatforming
current_form: high-touch Splunk rescue and cloud migration engagement
hidden_wedge: automation pack for hardening and validating fragile Splunk estates
end_state_company: operational upgrade layer for legacy observability platforms
desperate_user: Splunk platform owner blamed for outages and migration risk
job: stabilize and move a brittle Splunk estate without business disruption
workaround: specialist consultants, heroic upgrades, and ad hoc runbooks
reframing_direction: broad services migration to narrow automation and reliability kit
crazy_upside: go-to modernization layer for aging observability estates
reason_alive: Pain is real, but it still looks too project-shaped to trust as a company.

label: Splunk to Dynatrace Migrator
current_form: AI-powered asset conversion engine for one migration path
hidden_wedge: upload-and-convert observability asset translator
end_state_company: conversion engine for switching between monitoring platforms
desperate_user: platform lead staring at thousands of dashboards to rewrite
buyer: head of observability or platform engineering
job: translate dashboards and alerts faster than a manual migration team can
workaround: human translation, spreadsheet tracking, and parallel platform operation
reframing_direction: consulting accelerator to productized asset conversion engine
crazy_upside: neutral migration layer every observability vendor uses to win switchers
reason_alive: The wedge is crisp but time-bounded unless it broadens beyond one route.

label: Sybase Datadog Integration
current_form: Datadog plugin for monitoring Sybase ASE
hidden_wedge: legacy-database observability catalog
end_state_company: niche monitoring layer for orphaned but critical enterprise systems
desperate_user: database operations lead managing many aging Sybase estates
job: see critical Sybase health and audit data inside the modern observability stack
workaround: SSH scripts, separate dashboards, and disconnected alerts
reframing_direction: single marketplace integration to broader long-tail legacy monitoring suite
crazy_upside: control point for legacy systems modern platforms ignore
reason_alive: Real pain but small and shrinking market keeps this in faint-pulse territory.

label: Elastic Integration Hub
current_form: open-source integration catalog for Elastic
hidden_wedge: one-click onboarding for the hardest external data sources
end_state_company: cross-source ingestion and normalization layer around Elastic-like platforms
desperate_user: platform engineer maintaining brittle ingestion pipelines into Elastic
buyer: head of data platform or security engineering
job: get external data into Elastic quickly with usable schemas and dashboards
workaround: custom pipelines, raw ingest, or skipped sources entirely
reframing_direction: vendor catalog work to differentiated hard-source onboarding product
crazy_upside: default import layer for unstructured enterprise data into analytical systems
reason_alive: There is some option value in hard-source onboarding, but first-party absorption is strong.
